method_atom: an asymptotic identifiability analysis for recovering which few of many latent variables are non-zero (the support pattern) from a limited number of random noisy linear projections, deriving a matching pair of bounds — an algorithm-independent information-theoretic necessary condition (a lower bound on the number of measurements) and a sufficient condition achieved by a simple correlation/matched-filter estimator that thresholds the projection of the data onto each candidate direction — with the required measurement count scaling as the product of the number of active components and the logarithm of the ambient dimension, and constants fixed by the noise-to-signal ratio and the spread of component magnitudes.
object_atom: a k-sparse vector in R^n whose non-zero locations must be detected from m random noisy linear measurements, as arising in compressed sensing, system identification, denoising, and pattern recognition.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the necessary/sufficient sample-complexity machinery and matched-correlation estimator for identifying an active subset from random noisy linear observations; the domain-specific part is the sparse measurement vector of compressed sensing.
